title: celosia spicata var. holostachya
intro: Celosia spicata var. holostachya, also known as Cockscomb, is a variety of the Celosia spicata plant that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family, characterized by its vibrant red, pink, or yellow inflorescences resembling the comb of a rooster. Traditionally, Cockscomb has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in many cultures. Cockscomb can be prepared as a tea, poultice, or infusion, with the dried flowers or leaves typically used in herbal preparations, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
